expectations.text: Does not re-add configure() or agentic_session() that are already correctly wired — skips already-done work. Fixing/improving existing wiring (e.g. removing unnecessary guards) is acceptable.
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly documents existing configure() and agentic_session() as already present; the agent moved configure() to module level and removed the unnecessary if-guard — an improvement, not a re-addition.

expectations.text: Proposes at least one additional coded signal beyond what's already in place
expectations.evidence: Five new signals added: user-rate-limited (both endpoints), session-expired, session-turn-depth (METRIC), agent-stream-error, and user-retry replacing the weaker user-correction heuristic.

expectations.text: Proposes more synthetic evaluators than the default Task Completion alone
expectations.evidence: Four synthetic evaluators proposed: Task Completion, RAG Faithfulness, Conversation Completeness, and Knowledge Retention — three beyond the default Task Completion.

expectations.text: Does not propose adding a new React frontend or installing React from scratch — app has no React frontend. Extending an existing React setup if found in the repo is acceptable.
expectations.evidence: Transcript confirms 'Server-only, no frontend' and verification checklist notes 'No secret key in frontend (no React frontend)'; no VoteFeedback or React installation proposed anywhere in the diff or transcript.

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: Each signal has a dedicated rationale: user-rate-limited explains correlation with session patterns, session-expired explains context-loss failure mode, session-turn-depth explains it as a proxy for response quality, and each evaluator in the table has an explicit rationale column.
